message:
Refactor btrfs regular-file leaf test builder into small helpers

`write_test_btrfs_regular_file_tree` had grown into a
`too_many_arguments`/`too_many_lines`-suppressed monolith that
constructed all four leaf payloads (root inode, dir index, file
inode, extent), wrote the leaf items, and copied bytes into the leaf
buffer in one body.

This change splits the payload construction from the leaf layout:
- `BtrfsRegularFileLeafPayloads` groups the four payload byte vectors
  so the leaf writer can address them by field instead of juggling
  parallel locals.
- `build_test_btrfs_regular_file_leaf_payloads` owns the
  payload construction and is where the file size, dir index, and
  extent are derived from the caller's inputs.
- `make_test_btrfs_inode_item` centralizes the inode boilerplate
  (generation/times/uid/gid) that used to be duplicated for the root
  and file inodes; only `mode`, `size`, and `nlink` vary.
- `copy_into_leaf` replaces the four repeated `image[start..start+len]
  .copy_from_slice(...)` blocks that wrote payloads into the leaf at
  fixed data offsets.

The behavior and on-disk layout are unchanged; the
`too_many_lines` allow is dropped and `too_many_arguments` is now
the only remaining suppression. No production code is touched — this
is strictly test-helper refactoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
